1: theme Praise the LORD! Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in his mighty heavens! Praise in the sanctuary and mighty heavens — Hebrews 9:11-12: Christ appeared as a high priest of the good things that have come... he entered once for all into the holy places, not by means of the blood of goats and calves but by means of his own blood. The heavenly sanctuary is entered by Christ; Hebrews 10:19-20 gives the church boldness to enter through the curtain — Christ's body. The praise of Psalms 150:1 is offered in the true sanctuary Christ has opened.
2: shadow Praise him for his mighty deeds; praise him according to his excellent greatness! Mighty deeds and excellent greatness — the resurrection as the supreme mighty deed. Ephesians 1:19-20: the immeasurable greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to the working of his great might that he worked in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places. The excellent greatness of Psalms 150:2 is the greatness displayed in the resurrection of Christ.
3: shadow Praise him with trumpet sound; praise him with lute and harp! Trumpet, lute, and harp — the instruments of the new creation worship. 1 Corinthians 15:52: at the last trumpet... the dead will be raised imperishable. Revelation 8:2: the seven angels with seven trumpets. Revelation 5:8: the elders with harps. The instrumental praise of Ps 150 is the rehearsal for the eschatological worship at the sound of the last trumpet and the new song of the Lamb.
4: shadow Praise him with tambourine and dance; praise him with strings and pipe! Tambourine, dance, strings, and pipe — the full-bodied, whole-person worship. Luke 15:25 — the music and dancing at the prodigal's return foreshadows the eschatological celebration at the ingathering of lost humanity. Zephaniah 3:17: The LORD your God is in your midst, a mighty one who will save; he will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.
5: shadow Praise him with sounding cymbals; praise him with loud clashing cymbals! Loud clashing cymbals — the overwhelming, full-volume praise of the redeemed. Revelation 19:1-6: I heard what seemed to be the loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, crying out, Hallelujah!... like the roar of many waters and like the sound of mighty peals of thunder. The cymbals of Ps 150 are swallowed up in the thunderous Hallelujah of Revelation — the crescendo of all praise converging on the Lamb.
6: direct Let everything that has breath praise the LORD! Praise the LORD! Everything that has breath — Revelation 5:13: every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea, and all that is in them, saying, To him who sits on the throne and to the Lamb be blessing and honor and glory and might forever and ever! The Psalter closes with this universal summons; Revelation shows its fulfillment — all breathing things praise the Father and the Lamb together, the final word of all Scripture's worship.
